generated_sql.sql: WITH "count_a" AS (SELECT "employees"."employee_id" AS "entity_id", COUNT(DISTINCT "alerts"."alert_type") AS "count_a" FROM "employees" INNER JOIN "devices" ON "employees"."employee_id" = "devices"."employee_id" INNER JOIN "alerts" ON "devices"."device_id" = "alerts"."device_id" GROUP BY "employees"."employee_id"), "count_b" AS (SELECT "employees"."employee_id" AS "entity_id", COUNT(DISTINCT "alerts"."alert_type") AS "count_b" FROM "employees" INNER JOIN "incidents" ON "employees"."employee_id" = "incidents"."opened_by_employee_id" INNER JOIN "incident_alerts" ON "incidents"."incident_id" = "incident_alerts"."incident_id" INNER JOIN "alerts" ON "incident_alerts"."alert_id" = "alerts"."alert_id" GROUP BY "employees"."employee_id") SELECT "count_a"."entity_id" FROM "count_a" INNER JOIN "count_b" ON "count_a"."entity_id" = "count_b"."entity_id" WHERE "count_a"."count_a" > "count_b"."count_b"

full_response.relational_algebra: π count_a.entity_id (σ count_a.count_a > count_b.count_b ((((employees ⋈ employees.employee_id = devices.employee_id devices) ⋈ devices.device_id = alerts.device_id alerts) ⋈ alerts.alert_id = incident_alerts.alert_id incident_alerts) ⋈ employees.employee_id = incidents.opened_by_employee_id incidents))

question: Find devices with no vulnerabilities but with at least one high severity alert.
query_family_reason: no distinctive relational pattern; use base pipeline
sql: SELECT "devices"."device_id", "devices"."hostname", COUNT(*) AS "vuln_count" FROM "devices" INNER JOIN "alerts" ON "devices"."device_id" = "alerts"."device_id" INNER JOIN "device_vulnerabilities" ON "devices"."device_id" = "device_vulnerabilities"."device_id" WHERE "alerts"."severity" = ? AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM "device_vulnerabilities" WHERE "devices"."device_id" = "device_vulnerabilities"."device_id") GROUP BY "devices"."device_id", "devices"."hostname" HAVING "vuln_count" = ?

question: List employees where every device they own has been patched after all vulnerabilities on that device were detected.
query_family_reason: has/contains ALL members of a set (count-distinct division)
sql: SELECT DISTINCT "employees"."employee_id", "employees"."employee_name" FROM "devices" INNER JOIN "device_vulnerabilities" ON "devices"."device_id" = "device_vulnerabilities"."device_id" INNER JOIN "employees" ON "devices"."employee_id" = "employees"."employee_id" INNER JOIN "vulnerabilities" ON "device_vulnerabilities"."vulnerability_id" = "vulnerabilities"."vulnerability_id"

question: Find departments where the same manager supervises both the highest risk and lowest risk employee in that department.
query_family_reason: same entity has records at both the min and max within a group
sql: SELECT "employees"."manager_id", "employees"."department", MAX("employees"."risk_score") AS "max_risk", MIN("employees"."risk_score") AS "min_risk" FROM "employees" GROUP BY "employees"."department", "employees"."manager_id" HAVING "max_risk" = "min_risk"
